text: {"qa_pairs":[{"ordinal":1,"analyst_name":"Brent Thill","question_text":"Satya, can you give us a sense of how much of the Azure growth this quarter came specifically from AI workloads versus core compute? Investors are trying to triangulate the durability of the 39 percent Azure number.","answer_text":"Satya Nadella. Brent, in the quarter approximately 14 points of Azure growth came from our AI services, with the remaining 25 points coming from core consumption growth across compute, storage, and data services. That mix has been remarkably consistent for the last three quarters. The core business is healthy on its own merits, and AI is genuinely additive demand from customers who in many cases are net new to Azure.","answer_class":"direct"},{"ordinal":2,"analyst_name":"Keith Weiss","question_text":"Amy, the capex step-up from 88 billion to roughly 100 billion is significant. How should we think about the ROI window on that investment, and at what point would you slow the pace if returns are not materialising?","answer_text":"Amy Hood. Keith, we look at it through two lenses. First, we already have signed contractual commitments from customers that cover a meaningful share of the capacity coming online over the next 18 months. Second, the per-rack economics of our next-generation AI hardware are improving with each cohort we deploy. We expect AI gross margins to expand by roughly 200 basis points in fiscal 2027 relative to fiscal 2026, even before accounting for any pricing actions. If demand softens we have the optionality to slow incremental buildout within a quarter, but right now the constraint is supply not demand.","answer_class":"partial"},{"ordinal":3,"analyst_name":"Mark Moerdler","question_text":"On the security side, can you talk about the integration of the Charm AI acquisition you closed in October? Specifically, when do you expect that technology to be embedded across the Defender product family, and what kind of revenue uplift are you modelling?","answer_text":"Satya Nadella. Mark, we are pleased with the integration progress. The Charm AI team is now fully embedded within our Security division. We are not yet ready to disclose specific revenue impact numbers from the acquisition. What I can tell you is that we expect the underlying agentic capabilities to be generally available across Defender for Cloud, Defender for Endpoint, and Sentinel by the end of calendar 2026.","answer_class":"partial"},{"ordinal":4,"analyst_name":"Mark Murphy","question_text":"There has been increasing competitive noise from Google and Amazon around custom silicon for AI training. How is Microsoft thinking about its own Maia and Cobalt roadmap, and are you seeing customers explicitly request alternatives to Nvidia today?","answer_text":"Satya Nadella. Mark, our strategy on silicon is portfolio-based. We will continue to be the largest deployer of Nvidia GPUs at hyperscale, and we will continue to invest in Maia and Cobalt where the workload economics favor first-party silicon. On customer demand, we are seeing meaningful interest in Maia for inference workloads at large enterprise accounts, but most training is still pulling Nvidia. We will have more to share on the next-generation Maia roadmap at our Ignite conference next month.","answer_class":"partial"},{"ordinal":5,"analyst_name":"Karl Keirstead","question_text":"Amy, Commercial bookings were up 18 percent. Can you give us any granularity on duration or any large pull-forwards in the quarter?","answer_text":"Amy Hood. Karl, the bookings strength was broad-based and not driven by any single mega-deal. Contract duration ticked up by approximately three months versus prior year as customers committed to longer Azure consumption agreements to lock in capacity. We do not expect Q2 bookings to maintain this growth rate on a like-for-like basis given the prior-year compare, but the underlying RPO trajectory remains very healthy. We will provide more color on RPO at our Q2 call in January.","answer_class":"partial"},{"ordinal":6,"analyst_name":"Brad Sills","question_text":"On Copilot for Microsoft 365, the 78,000 paid customer milestone is impressive. Can you talk about what is driving conversion from trial to paid right now, and specifically whether you are seeing any pricing pressure in mid-market accounts?","answer_text":"Satya Nadella. Brad, conversion velocity has actually accelerated since we shipped the Copilot Studio and Copilot Pages updates in September. The single biggest driver is customers building their own workflow agents on top of the base Copilot license. On pricing, we have not made any list-price changes and we are not seeing material discount pressure in mid-market.","answer_class":"direct"},{"ordinal":7,"analyst_name":"Kirk Materne","question_text":"Quick clarifying question on the gaming segment. Activision contribution to gaming revenue this quarter, and any expectation for when Game Pass will return to growth?","answer_text":"Amy Hood. Kirk, gaming revenue was 5.1 billion dollars in the quarter, with Activision contributing approximately 1.9 billion of that. Game Pass subscriber growth resumed sequentially in the quarter, but we are not providing a specific re-acceleration timeline. We expect to share more on our gaming strategy at the next investor update.","answer_class":"partial"}],"commitments":[{"commitment_text":"Microsoft expects to bring an incremental 1.7 gigawatts of data-center capacity online by the end of fiscal 2026.","target_period":"FY2026","source_quote":"We expect to bring an incremental 1.7 gigawatts of capacity online by the end of fiscal 2026, on top of what we delivered last year."},{"commitment_text":"Full-year capital expenditure (including finance leases) is expected to land in the $95 to $100 billion range, up from prior guidance of approximately $88 billion.","target_period":"FY2026","source_quote":"we now expect full-year capex to land in the 95 to 100 billion dollar range, up from our prior guidance of approximately 88 billion dollars."},{"commitment_text":"AI gross margins are expected to expand by roughly 200 basis points in fiscal 2027 relative to fiscal 2026, before any pricing actions.","target_period":"FY2027","source_quote":"We expect AI gross margins to expand by roughly 200 basis points in fiscal 2027 relative to fiscal 2026, even before accounting for any pricing actions."},{"commitment_text":"Charm AI agentic capabilities will be generally available across Defender for Cloud, Defender for Endpoint, and Sentinel by the end of calendar 2026.","target_period":"Q4 2026","source_quote":"we expect the underlying agentic capabilities to be generally available across Defender for Cloud, Defender for Endpoint, and Sentinel by the end of calendar 2026."},{"commitment_text":"Microsoft will share more detail on the next-generation Maia silicon roadmap at the Ignite conference next month.","target_period":"next 12 months","source_quote":"We will have more to share on the next-generation Maia roadmap at our Ignite conference next month."},{"commitment_text":"Microsoft does not expect Q2 bookings to maintain the Q1 18% growth rate on a like-for-like basis given the prior-year compare.","target_period":"Q2 2026","source_quote":"We do not expect Q2 bookings to maintain this growth rate on a like-for-like basis given the prior-year compare, but the underlying RPO trajectory remains very healthy."},{"commitment_text":"Microsoft will provide more color on RPO at the Q2 earnings call in January.","target_period":"Q2 2026","source_quote":"We will provide more color on RPO at our Q2 call in January."}]}
